book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
The Imperial Edict Posted in the Third Atrium of the Great Church Near What is Called Dicymbala.
title: The Imperial Edict Posted in the Third Atrium of the Great Church Near What is Called Dicymbala.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: In the name of our Lord and Master Jesus Christ, our God and Saviour, the most pious Emperor, the peaceful and Christ-loving Constantine, an Emperor faithful to God in Jesus Christ, to all our Christ-loving people living in this God-preserved and royal city.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Hefele’s Epitome (Hist. of the Councils, Vol. v., p.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: “The heresy of Apollinaris, etc., has been renewed by Theodore of Pharan and confirmed by Honorius, sometime Pope of Old Rome, who also contradicted himself.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Also Cyrus, Pyrrhus, Paul, Peter; more recently.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Macarius, Stephen, and Polychronius had diffused Monothelitism.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: He, the Emperor, had therefore convoked this holy and Ecumenical Synod, and published the present edict with the confession of faith, in order to confirm and establish its decrees.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: (There follows here an extended confession of faith, with proofs for the doctrine of two wills and operations.)

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: As he recognized the five earlier Ecumenical Synods, so he anathematized all heretics from Simon Magus, but especially the originator and patrons of the new heresy, Theodore and Sergius; also Pope Honorius, who was their adherent and patron in everything, and confirmed the heresy (τὸν κατὰ πάντα τούτοις συναιρέτην καὶ σύνδρομον καὶ βεβαιωτὴν τῆς αἱρέσεως, further, Cyrus, etc., and ordained that no one henceforth should hold a different faith, or venture to teach one will and one energy.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: In no other than the orthodox faith could men be saved.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Whoever did not obey the imperial edict should, if he were a bishop or cleric be deposed; if an official, punished with confiscation of property and loss of the girdle (ζώνη); if a private person, banished from the residence and all other cities.”
